failure_modes.text: Teams can keep hitting activity targets long after it is obvious that the outcome is not moving.
failure_modes.source_quote: input-vs-output-goals conflicts with commitment-bias (commitment bias) when teams keep activity targets despite weak outcome movement.
failure_modes.mitigation: Check whether activity targets are still connected to the outcome; hitting input goals after the output stops moving is waste.

failure_modes.text: Reward design can over-index on visible effort and hide whether value is actually being delivered.
failure_modes.source_quote: input-vs-output-goals conflicts with incentives (incentives) when reward design over-indexes on visible effort instead of value delivery.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit reward design for effort-visibility bias: visible activity is easy to measure but may hide whether value is actually being delivered.

failure_modes.text: People can fixate on outcomes they cannot directly control and never design the behaviors that would make progress repeatable.
failure_modes.source_quote: The model is strongest when people can state the desired result yet still fail to translate that result into repeatable behaviors, habits, and checkpoints.
failure_modes.mitigation: Design the input behaviors that would cause the outcome rather than fixating on outcomes the team cannot directly control.

premortem_questions.text: Which of our current input metrics would still look good even if the real outcome kept deteriorating?
premortem_questions.source_quote: activity metrics are abundant but the link between action and outcome is weak or obscured

premortem_questions.text: What repeatable behavior do we control directly that should move this outcome if our theory is right?
premortem_questions.source_quote: translate that result into repeatable behaviors, habits, and checkpoints

premortem_questions.text: Are we rewarding effort theater or real value delivery?
premortem_questions.source_quote: reward design over-indexes on visible effort instead of value delivery

heuristics.text: Translate the desired result into behaviors and checkpoints the team can actually control.
Track input metrics only when there is an explicit causal claim connecting them to the outcome.
heuristics.source_quote: clear metrics tied to the Output Goal is crucial for knowing if the process (Input) is working

heuristics.text: Use process goals to reduce anxiety around distant outcomes while still testing whether the process produces value.
heuristics.source_quote: Focusing on the process helps reduce anxiety and stress associated with overthinking the outcome.

curation_notes.summary: Focused on translating desired outcomes into controllable process moves and exposing activity metrics that hide weak outcomes.
donor_drops: Dropped broader donor-style elaboration in favor of three compact source-grounded intervention chunks per type.
open_questions: Relation richness stays in Wave 3; Wave 2 only preserves the intervention surface needed for preview substrate use.

allies: Wave 3 preserves the first-order ally surface where the raw source gives a clean current-vocabulary seam.
antagonists: Wave 3 preserves the strongest current-vocabulary conflicts and leaves weaker cross-model seams omitted on purpose.
structured_tensions: Wave 4 remains observational only; this batch does not widen higher-order source coverage.
